title: I really feel like an idiot
display_title: I really feel like an idiot
wikitext:
[]: I really feel like an idiot. Post by EVA X » Sun Jan 10, 2010 5:17 am So, first off, prior to reading all the articles here, all the info I got about NGE was from the anime series, and EOE. Which still had me pretty confused. Now...for the longest time, I had thought that NGE was basically...supernatural and what not, with souls, giant energy crosses, and so on. Until I read the article about the First Ancestral Race, I mean...it all started with aliens? Adam, Lilith, White Moon, Black Moon, Dead Sea Scrolls, and the Lance of Longinus...I just really didn't take time to think that it all came from aliens. Talk about FAIL huh?

[]: Post by child of Lilith » Sun Jan 10, 2010 5:21 am Don’t worry about it, nobody’s perfect. At lest you took the time to check your facts.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 10, 2010 5:31 am The unashamed use of Christian and Qaballistic imagery tends to mislead people -- especially in a not particularly secular society like much of the US -- who don't realise that GAINAX started off as a bunch of scf-fi fans doing promo work for the national SF conventions. The clues are there in series, but you have to look at them knowing that it's all coming from an SF mindset.

[]: Post by EVA X » Sun Jan 10, 2010 5:36 am Mr. Tines wrote: The unashamed use of Christian and Qaballistic imagery tends to mislead people -- especially in a not particularly secular society like much of the US -- who don't realise that GAINAX started off as a bunch of scf-fi fans doing promo work for the national SF conventions. The clues are there in series, but you have to look at them knowing that it's all coming from an SF mindset.

[]: Post by Allemann » Sun Jan 10, 2010 8:38 am If you really want to, you can interpreted some elements as undeveloped kernels of christian existentialism.

[]: Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Jan 10, 2010 10:07 am Allemann wrote: undeveloped kernels of christian existentialism.

[]: Re: I really feel like an idiot. Post by J_Faulkner » Sun Jan 10, 2010 10:20 am Mr. Tines wrote: The unashamed use of Christian and Qaballistic imagery tends to mislead people

[]: Post by scarmullet » Sun Jan 10, 2010 10:21 am I dunno, There are a lot of links between EVA and Revelatons. Stuff like twelve beasts and the final one looking like a man and such.

[]: Post by J_Faulkner » Sun Jan 10, 2010 10:33 am scarmullet wrote: I dunno, There are a lot of links between EVA and Revelatons.

[]: Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Jan 10, 2010 10:35 am scarmullet wrote: Stuff like twelve beasts and the final one looking like a man and such.

[]: Re: I really feel like an idiot. Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Jan 10, 2010 10:50 am J_Faulkner wrote: and it's all the more embarrassing that the creators consciously included them to look "cool".

[]: Post by Dartz » Sun Jan 10, 2010 12:32 pm I always thought panspermia to be a cool idea.... that we might have distant relative floating about somewhere out there. It's also kind of cool that it suggests the Abramic religions were inspired by the FAR directly somehow.

[]: Post by scarmullet » Sun Jan 10, 2010 1:36 pm J_Faulkner wrote: scarmullet wrote: I dunno, There are a lot of links between EVA and Revelatons.

[]: Post by J_Faulkner » Sun Jan 10, 2010 1:42 pm scarmullet wrote: I think it was done purposefully to set it apart. Like star wars has some tie ins with older stories, that doesn't mean that Star Wars is related to those stories.

[]: Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Jan 10, 2010 1:45 pm J_Faulkner wrote: Why doesn't he include things from other religions like Hinduism or Islam in Rebuild?

[]: Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 10, 2010 1:51 pm Eva X: Don't worry about it, man. When I first joined here over three years ago (I was thirteen), I didn't know a damned thing about any of the obscure stuff. I was like "WHOA COOL GIANT BIRD THINGY!" "WHOA COOL GIANT PURPLE MAN WITH A HORN!" "WHOA COOL TEH NEKKID Rei!".

[]: Post by J_Faulkner » Sun Jan 10, 2010 2:04 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: Because they're not as cool as Christian symbols. Actually that could be the real answer. He probably felt that other religious symbolism wasn't needed.

[]: Post by Ornette » Sun Jan 10, 2010 2:11 pm This thread is lame, bump one of the appropriate threads of relevant discussion.
